_comment: Set OPENAI_API_KEY and OPENAI_BASE_URL in .env. Put all benchmark and judge model IDs here. Model IDs must be supported by your OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

object_counting._comment: Use structured_output=true only for models/endpoints that support OpenAI response_format=json_schema. Otherwise use false.
object_counting.benchmark_dir: data/Use Case 1 - Object Counting/1 - Full Datasets

models.name: OpenAI GPT-5.5
models.note: Replace model_id with a vision-capable model supported by your OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

models.name: OpenAI GPT-5.5
models.note: Ablation template: internal step-by-step counting, final JSON only.

models.name: OpenAI GPT-5.5
models.note: Ablation template: one-call internal draft plus self-check, final JSON only.

models.name: OpenAI GPT-5.5
models.note: Ablation template: two API calls per image; first count, then reflection/correction.

qa._comment: All enabled models here are asked visual QA questions with image_url data URLs.
qa.images_dir: data/Use Case 2 - Drawing Understanding/01 - Full Dataset/images
qa.labels_dir: data/Use Case 2 - Drawing Understanding/01 - Full Dataset/labels

models.name: API Gemini 3.1 Pro
models.note: Replace model_id with a vision-capable model supported by your OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

models.note: Ablation template: internal step-by-step QA, final concise answer only.

models.name: API Gemini 3.1 Pro
models.note: Ablation template: one-call internal draft plus self-check, final concise answer only.

models.name: API Gemini 3.1 Pro
models.note: Ablation template: two API calls per question; initial answer then reflection/correction.

judge._comment: This model evaluates QA result CSV files. It does not need vision input, but should be strong at text judgment.
